root_text.an6.74:1.1: “Cha, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharituṁ.
root_text.an6.74:1.3: Kāmavitakkaṁ, byāpādavitakkaṁ, vihiṁsāvitakkaṁ, kāmasaññaṁ, byāpādasaññaṁ, vihiṁsāsaññaṁ—
root_text.an6.74:1.4: ime kho, bhikkhave, cha dhamme appahāya abhabbo paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharituṁ.
root_text.an6.74:2.1: Cha, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharituṁ.
root_text.an6.74:2.3: Kāmavitakkaṁ, byāpādavitakkaṁ, vihiṁsāvitakkaṁ, kāmasaññaṁ, byāpādasaññaṁ, vihiṁsāsaññaṁ—
root_text.an6.74:2.4: ime kho, bhikkhave, cha dhamme pahāya bhabbo paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharitun”ti.

translation_text.an6.74:1.1: “Mendicants, without giving up these six qualities you can’t enter and remain in the first absorption.
translation_text.an6.74:1.3: Sensual, malicious, and cruel thoughts; and sensual, malicious, and cruel perceptions.
translation_text.an6.74:1.4: Without giving up these six qualities you can’t enter and remain in the first absorption.
translation_text.an6.74:2.1: But after giving up these six qualities you can enter and remain in the first absorption.
translation_text.an6.74:2.3: Sensual, malicious, and cruel thoughts; and sensual, malicious, and cruel perceptions.
translation_text.an6.74:2.4: After giving up these six qualities you can enter and remain in the first absorption.”
